name: Miroslav Vitouš
description: Czech bass guitarist, jazz musician and contrabassist
abstract: Miroslav Ladislav Vitouš (born 6 December 1947) is a Czech jazz bassist. He is known as a founding member of the ensemble Weather Report, and for working as a bandleader and alongside Chick Corea, Jack DeJohnette and others.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Weather Report, Roy Haynes Hip Ensemble, Tony Williams's Lifetime, and the Archie Schepp Quintet
Kool Jazz Festival: A Night of Genius-Artists Celebrate Mingus

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: I had just met Miroslav, and we were doing some more free kind of stuff together... putting Miroslav together with Roy... So we were in the studio... Everything was a first take.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Haynes
about_quotes.evidence: So, getting back to Chick. Chick – okay, we did that record, Now He Sings, Now He Sobs. We didn’t get credit. “We” meaning the bass player and myself, Miroslav Vitous.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy Haynes — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: Miroslav was in the band for a short while when we were – we played in Chicago
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hide Tanaka
about_quotes.evidence: But also at that time, the seventies, there were so many super bass players that came out. Stanley Clarke, Miroslav Vitous, Neils Henning, Jordan [inaudible], Eddie Gomez.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hide Tanaka interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 3, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: Akiyoshi Toshiko
about_quotes.evidence: in the afternoon I was put into a trio with Elvin Jones, and then it was, uh, Miroslav [Vitous] had just come from
about_quotes.doc_title: Akiyoshi Toshiko — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] and Miroslav Vitous. [INTERVIEWEE] Now He Sings, Now He Sobs. I thought that was a fabulous record.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
